signals.idea: Add audio-reactive effects to the video editor. Effects should be able to respond to audio data (frequency bins, amplitude, beat detection) from a blended mix of all audio tracks in the timeline. This must integrate with the existing effects infrastructure: the DynamicEffectRegistry, the compileEffect.tsx runtime compilation (inject audio hooks as new globals alongside useCurrentFrame/interpolate/etc), the effectPromptTemplate.ts (so AI-generated effects can use audio data), and the ParameterControls system (new audio-binding parameter type). Key integration points: (1) Audio analysis layer that extracts frequency/amplitude/beat data from a mixed-down version of all timeline audio tracks, synchronized to Remotion's frame clock. (2) A useAudioReactive() hook (or similar) injected into compiled custom effects via the new Function() call in compileEffect.tsx. (3) Updated effectPromptTemplate.ts documenting the new audio globals. (4) The continuous effect category is the primary target but entrance/exit effects should also be able to use audio data. (5) Preview support in EffectCreatorPanel.tsx. The existing effect wrapping pipeline (wrapWithEffect/wrapWithClipEffects in effects/index.tsx -> VisualClip.tsx -> EffectLayerSequence.tsx) should remain unchanged - audio data flows through the hook, not through props.

unresolved_flags.concern: video-editor-effect-generation: The plan updates a legacy prompt template but misses the live edge-generator prompt and parameter-schema definitions used by `EffectCreatorPanel`.

video-editor-audio-analysis: The proposed offline-analysis design is technically underspecified because `OfflineAudioContext` rendering does not give deterministic per-frame FFT data through `AnalyserNode` as written.
video-editor-render-pipeline: Async audio fetching and decoding would race Remotion preview and export because the current composition path has no render-delay handling.
video-editor-parameter-schema: `audio-binding` needs object-valued defaults and display and validation support in multiple duplicated type definitions, not just a new union member.

video-editor-effect-params: The plan adds `audio-binding` as a second runtime contract, but current callers still pass `params` through unchanged and the plan does not define how bindings become usable effect behavior.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the live effect-creation flow in `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:297-310`; it generates effects through the `ai-generate-effect` edge function, whose prompt contract and parameter schema types live in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts:3-20,53-75`. The plan only calls out `src/tools/video-editor/effects/effectPromptTemplate.ts`, so it does not fully address the repository's actual AI-generation path for documenting `useAudioReactive()` or `audio-binding`.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked preview integration in `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:141-176,485-495`. The Remotion tree is created inside `makePreviewComposition()` and then passed as `component={PreviewComposition}` to `<Player>`, so the provider has to be inserted inside the returned composition component; wrapping the outer Player element would not satisfy the requirement.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the parameter types and defaults in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:13-23`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx:16-48`, and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts:5-11`. All three currently hard-code `number | string | boolean`, so adding an `audio-binding` object value requires widening those signatures; the plan mentions new cases but does not explicitly fix the existing annotations that would reject `{ source, min, max }` defaults and display values.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `validateAndCoerceParams()` in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts:22-31`. The final `else` branch is color validation, so any new parameter type not handled before that branch gets silently coerced to the color fallback; `audio-binding` therefore needs an explicit branch before the color fallback or object values will be discarded at runtime.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the proposed offline-analysis mechanism against the current repository and Remotion render path. There is no existing FFT helper in the repo, `OfflineAudioContext` plus `AnalyserNode` is not enough for deterministic per-frame offline extraction, `ResolvedTimelineConfig.output` has no duration field in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx:100-162` / `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:30-38`, and the repo has no `delayRender`/`continueRender` handling, so the provider design is underspecified for correct preview/export behavior.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Searched for parameter-schema handling across the video editor and edge generator. Beyond the frontend files named in the plan, `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts:3-20,61-75` duplicates `ParameterType` and `ParameterDefinition` and teaches the model which parameter types are valid, so the underlying change is broader than the listed frontend scope.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked where parameter defaults are consumed with `rg getDefaultValues`. `EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:199-201,324-379`, `ClipPanel.tsx:76-93,261-408,443-456`, and `BulkClipPanel.tsx:86` all depend on the same default-value behavior, so adding `audio-binding` affects creation, editing, and application paths across the feature rather than only `ParameterControls.tsx`.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked all prompt-template locations. The repository has both `src/tools/video-editor/effects/effectPromptTemplate.ts` and the production generator prompt in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts`; the plan only names the first file, so it misses supporting infrastructure that actually drives `EffectCreatorPanel` generation.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked preview-call plumbing in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:436-440` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:182-190`. The panel currently receives only `previewAssetSrc` and `timelineFps`, not audio clips or timeline config, so any non-synthetic preview strategy based on real timeline audio needs additional caller and prop changes that the plan does not list.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked supporting automated infrastructure around the generator. The edge function already has tests in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/index.test.ts`, so if the prompt contract or parameter schema changes, that test surface is part of the touched locations even though it is not listed in the plan.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked every caller of `wrapWithEffect()` in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:124-184` and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:35-48`. All callers pass `params` through as raw `Record<string, unknown>`, so an `audio-binding` parameter will still arrive inside effect code as an object unless the plan also defines a helper or convention for resolving that object against `useAudioReactive()`; the current plan leaves that runtime contract underspecified.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `EffectCreatorPanel` callers in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:436-440`. The caller arguments are limited to visual asset preview and FPS, which means the plan's optional actual-audio preview idea is not supported by current call sites and would need broader API changes than the plan states.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the listed `must` criteria against the planned verification. `EffectCreatorPanel preview works for audio-reactive effects` and `All 11 entrance, 6 exit, and 5 continuous built-in effects are unaffected` are reasonable goals, but as written they are not yet review-verifiable from the proposed automated checks and still depend on broad manual judgment unless the plan adds explicit automated assertions for preview behavior and built-in-effect coverage.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 21.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 18.

items.criterion: All existing DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx tests pass without modification
Compiled effects that do NOT call useAudioReactive() work identically to before (no behavioral change)
useAudioReactive() returns zeroed/silent AudioAnalysisData when no AudioAnalysisProvider exists in the tree
useAudioReactive() returns frame-synchronized audio data (indexed by useCurrentFrame()) when AudioAnalysisProvider is present
wrapWithEffect and wrapWithClipEffects in effects/index.tsx are not modified
EffectComponentProps type does not gain audio-related props
ParameterType union includes 'audio-binding' and ParameterControls renders corresponding UI
validateAndCoerceParams correctly validates and coerces audio-binding parameter values
effectPromptTemplate.ts documents useAudioReactive() with return type and usage examples
AudioAnalysisProvider wraps the composition in TimelineRenderer.tsx so all effects can access audio data
EffectCreatorPanel preview works for audio-reactive effects (synthetic signal when no audio present)
All 11 entrance, 6 exit, and 5 continuous built-in effects are unaffected
No new Remotion packages added — uses Web Audio API only
AudioAnalysisProvider new file is under ~300 lines
Beat detection uses energy-threshold with cooldown to avoid false positives
Audio analysis is pre-computed (offline) for deterministic rendering
Audio-binding parameter UI shows source selector (bass/mid/treble/amplitude) and min/max range controls
Manual smoke test: create a custom effect using useAudioReactive() in the effect creator, preview shows movement with synthetic audio
Manual smoke test: add audio track to timeline, apply audio-reactive continuous effect, playback shows effect responding to actual audio
